content:
What protocol have you used to connect to the server in each application (imap or pop3)?

  • K9 Mail
  • Thunderbird Android
  • Thunderbird desktop on Mac

imap keeps client(s) and server synchnonised. Deletions performed on a client app are deleted on the server. The deletion will also be applied on any other clients connecting using imap.

Pop3 doesn't support synchronisation. It downloads new emails and stores them locally on the client / device. When you delete an email, only the local app's copy is removed.

It sounds like you have K9 setup to use POP3 and Thunderbird for Android using Imap.

You can check which protocol the mobile apps are using by navigating to ... Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server

Server Name = Can very for imap and pop3 Port 993 = imap port 995 = pop3
creator: platform34

content:
Q1) Where does Thunderbird for Android save POP3 Mailboxes?

The mailbox data is stored within protected internal storage (app sandbox). It's not accessible unless you have a rooted phone.

Q2) Is there a Backup-Restore Feature for POP3 Mailboxes?

Thunderbird for Android doesn't currently offer any backup or restore mechanism for mailbox data.

You can add your idea(s) on Mozilla connect and receive votes from the community. The TB team take these into consideration when planning feature additions and roadmaps. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't support OAuth with pop3 connections. I suspect your desktop is using POP3 and being imported on TB mobile via QR code.

Imap is the modern and recommended protocol.

To setup Imap

You should setup the gmail account manually, instead of using the QR code. If your still on the first time setup wizard, tap 'get started' and skip step 1 & 2.

  1. Tap the top left menu button to open the side bar.
  2. Tap settings > add account.
  3. Enter your email address
  4. Imap settings will be automatically found & configured
  5. Click the sign-in with google button. Login to the webpage.
  6. Grant TB Mobile permission to access your emails.

To remove an account.

  1. Tap the top left menu button to open the side bar.
  2. Tap settings.
  3. Tap the account name.
  4. Tap the 3 dot menu (top right).
  5. Tap remove account.

To continue using pop3

You will need to setup an app password via gmail's webmail settings page. https://support.google.com/mail/answer/185833?hl=en

Once you have the app password ...

Follow step 1 to 4 above to add the account.

  1. On the configuration found box, click the down arrow.
  2. Click edit configuration.
  3. Change protocol = Pop
  4. Change server = pop.gmail.com
  5. Change port = 995
  6. Change authentication = normal password.
  7. Enter your new app password in the password field.
  8. Click next to complete the account setup.

You can view and edit the configuration of an exiting account by navigating to ...

  • Settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server
  • Settings > account name > sending mail > outgoing server

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't support the use of oAuth 2.0 with Pop connections. I suspect your desktop accounts are setup with this configuration and imported by QR code into the mobile app. This would explain the error you see.

If this is the case, you have two options.

  1. Continue using oAuth 2.0 authentication. You must change from Pop3 to an Imap connection.
  2. Continue using a Pop connection. You must ..

a. Generate an app password via your providers webmail settings. b. Change authentication type in TBA accounts from oAuth 2.0 to normal user name & password. c. Enter the newly generated app password in the password field.

Note: App passwords are a generated 16 digit passcode, not your normal email account password.

To check if Imap or pop is being used ... Navigate to settings > account name > fetching mail > incoming server.

Pop Server name = pop.gmail.com Port = 995

Imap Server name = imap.gmail.com Port = 993

Imap is the modern connection protocol, supporting sync on multiple devices.

content:
Hi Charalampos

The sync interval can't be reduced below 15 minutes. Google imposes this limit in Android for apps running background services such as TB's sync mechanism.

If you require for more timely notifications, you should enable the foreground push service. This is enabled per folder and provides immediate email delivery and notifications.

See the guide linked below. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/configure-push-email-thunderbird-android

To receive notification of emails arriving in folders you've added.

1. Enable the Notifications setting on the folder. 2. Enable the Sync or push setting on the folder.

To access these settings ...

1. Open the side menu with account folders showing 2. Tap manage folders (bottom of list) 3. Tap desired folder name 4. Toggle above settings ON

content:
Have you checked if the emails are found within your trash / deleted mail folder?

Did you delete the email(s) on your desktop client? Doing so would normally move them from inbox to the trash folder on the server. The mobile app would reflect this after synchronization.

To see deleted mails in the mobile app, you may need to enable sync on the folder.

  1. Open the side bar showing your account folders
  2. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  3. Tap your trash/deleted folder
  4. Toggle 'Enable Sync' ON
creator: platform34

content:
Hi Tom

Are your missing emails located in a custom folder rather than a default one such as Inbox? Sync is off by default on custom folders and may need enabling on each. This could be why some read/unread status changes are not updating in the app.

  1. Open the side menu to display your account folders
  2. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  3. Tap desired folder name
  4. Toggle 'Enable Sync' On

Are the missing emails particuarly large in size. You could try increasing the size limit of messages the app will fetch. Navigate to settings > account > fetching mail > fetch messages up to: 32 Kib (default)

You can turn on 'Sync Notifications' to see a silent notification in the Android status bar and drawer while sync is actively running. This will give you greater visibility of when it starts and completes.

Goto Settings > account name > notifications: Sync Notifications
creator: platform34

content:
Thanks for the reply. This is the inbox that isn't syncing. I did increase the size of the messages it would fetch, as well as the number of messages it would retain locally. However, it isn't just a handful of larger messages that are missing, it's anything newer than a certain time.

I also don't have the system set up to refresh in the background. I prefer to have it log in and fetch when I open the app (I'd rather wait a few moments when I open the app than have it burning power in the background all day).

While typing this, I extended the system delay before the screen auto-locks to 5-minutes and it did fetch newer e-mails. Looking at the screen, I can now see that the thermometer bar between the message list and the folder name at the top is still present and slowly progressing.

I'm going to consider this resolved, as my working theory is that I enabled a large number of messages to be retrieved and stored locally, including fairly large ones, and it simply hasn't finished fetching them. By turning off the background sync, I have made it such that it gets interrupted every time the screen locks. With any luck, once the initial retrieval has finished normal day-to-day sync operations will work as expected.

A better progress meter would be nice to see whether it's working (along the lines of the Activity Manager on Desktop), but then again, I'm clearly a corner case here. I'll also check the notification you mention, which may be what I'm looking for.

content:
The ability to see the full date and time was added in v18, which I believe your running.

Navigate to settings > general settings > display: Date Time Format. Assign either 'Full' or 'ISO'.

The change in picture display behaviour stems from a redesign of general attachment placement. They were moved to the top of emails making them more visible and accessible. This had the side effect that picture attachments are not automatically displayed.

A bug report is raised on github about this, linked below. https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10889

content:
The developers are aware of performance issues. Typically when the local folder size is set to a high number of messages or a large number of accounts are present. They are working on refactoring the app which should improve this is a future release.

If you have the local folder size set to a high number of messages, try reducing this. Settings > account name > fetching mail: local folder size

Reference: https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10373#issuecomment-3810077139

"The app's performance slows down when many messages are downloaded. This is because it currently loads all messages into memory at once."

"I tested this and performance begins to degrade noticeably once the message count exceeds 500. This is worse than tought, we need to improve the message handling. Propably database related, but the message list refactor could help too."

Reference: https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10409#issuecomment-3811149422

"We recognize that the performance and loading times of the Message List are not optimal. We are currently working on a refactor to ensure the app runs smoothly, even when managing multiple accounts."

content:
Q1) Notification did not clear even I deleted all the mail.

A bug a report is open to investigate the same point you raise. https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10936

Q2) No option to delete mail on trash on exit

Android apps generally don't offer an exit or quit option. The android eco-system is designed so the OS manages resources and terminates applications when resources are low, otherwise apps continue to run in the background.

In this context an "empty trash on exit" option is meaningless.

content:
The app does support displaying HTML emails.

The subject field is displayed as plain text however the body of your email is HTML. Otherwise there would be no inline images or text formatting: font type, size, weight (bold) etc.

You can toggle a message between HTML and plain text.

  1. Press the 3 dot menu in the tool bar.
  2. Select "Show unformatted message.

You can also set the default message format between HTML and plain text in the settings menu. Settings > general settings > display > Body Content-Type

content:
Hi Darlha

Are your TB desktop accounts setup to use Imap or the pop protocol to connect to the server. https://www.one.com/en-gb/email/what-is-imap-and-pop3/

Imap is the modern protocol designed for use on multiple devices. Emails and folders are stored on the server and synchronised with each device.

Pop is the legacy protocol designed for use on a single device. Emails are downloaded and stored locally on the device. Once downloaded they are typically deleted from the server. Folders and emails are NOT sychronised to other devices.

If you're using Imap, any folders you have on the server will automatically synchronise and show in Thunderbird for Android.

You should use your providers webmail to create any new folders as direct folder creation is unsupported within TB-A

To enable emails within a given folder to automatically synchronise,

  1. Open the side menu to show the accounts folder list (Inbox, Sent, Draft etc)
  2. Tap manage folders (bottom of list)
  3. Tap a folder name
  4. Toggle "Enable Sync" or "Push" ON

To refresh your Imap folder list from the server ... On the manage folders screen, tap the three dot menu (top right of screen) and select refresh folder list.

If your TB desktop is using POP, you can still use IMAP on your phone. Instead of importing your account configuration using the QRCode, you should manually add them to Thunderbird for Android using the setup wizard.

Any emails stored only on your TB Desktop POP account will need to be uploaded to the server before they will be available in the mobile app.
creator: platform34
